Intro: ^Welcome my friend, you look hungry! Care for the special roll? The best in town, you won't regret it, friend!
^But make no mistake. Extra sauce don't come free around here. I am being more than generous with the amount of sauce that I already put into each and every one of my rolls.
^You think sauce grows on trees, fucker? You think the Sauce Boy comes around giving free handouts? Get real.
^Some fuck ordered a vegan kebab. Who does that? I have a tendency towards violence and I considered killing the man right there and then.
^I could kill for less than that, actually. There's this guy that would always ask for extra...
^Anyway, did you know the victim then? The one who was found by the dumpster?
^Of course, he was my most frequent customer! Half the town knows the guy. You should be glad that you didn't, though.
BackStory: ^Around 3 years ago, a man I’ve never seen before came up to my stand and ordered the least sold item on my menu. I was ecstatic, for it was the crown jewel of all rolls, yet no one would ever order it.
^But this man, sent from above, by the grace of the gods, ordered it nonetheless. To show my gratitude, I would put extra sauce on it, free of charge.

^The man, in return, solemnly nodded his head, and went on his way without a single word. I had a big grin on my face the rest of the day, all the way back home later that night. I laid down in my bed, my smile not wavering.
^Suddenly, a feeling of dread washed over me. I felt a large pit in my stomach. I couldn’t sleep, and I could feel my smile slowly turning into something else. I was afraid, but I didn’t know why.
^The next day I opened up my shop as usual. The feeling wouldn’t go away, and it was only getting worse.
^Then I saw him, that same man from yesterday, rapidly approaching. I wanted to smile and wave at him.
^And that’s when it hit. That same feeling of dread from last night hit me like a truck, and I suddenly knew what it meant. This man was not sent from above.
^With each step he took towards me, the fear grew stronger. The loathing intensified. I tried feigning a smile, but it was pointless.
^The man, who I had held in such high regard, started demanding the cheapest roll, with as much extra sauce as possible. I was just standing there staring at him, jaw agape.

^“I want that extra sauce, I deserve it even.”, he said. “Is this how you treat your loyal customers, denying them their sauce? Can’t believe this shit. I won’t come back here, and I’ll make damn sure that no one else does either after I tell them about this”.
^And with that, he would come back every day. Order the cheapest roll. Get the most sauce on it, and never pay extra.
^It only took a few months for me to go into debt. Even if I pleaded with him, it only made things worse. This was my life now.
^I have no sauce, and I must scream.
IntroContinued: ^Sounds like you two had a 'saucy' relationship.
Confront: ^Hello, Boss Man. I found something interesting by the crime scene.
^THIS IS THE END OF THE DEMO SUPPORT THIS PROJECT BY DONATING TO OUR PATREON THANK YOU TO ALL OUR SUPPORTERS
